payload.query: Milestone 1: reduction from prime-free interval to p_{n+1}-p_n bound Milestone 2: valid CRT covering lemma with pairwise-coprime structure Milestone 3: parameter-growth lemma linking N, Q, and n Is it true that, for any $C>0$, there are infinitely many $n$ such that\[p_{n+1}-p_n> C\frac{\log\log n\log\log\log\log n}{(\log\log \log n)^2}\log n?\]

move.claim: â pairwise coprime q_1,â¦,q_k â¥ 2 with Q = â q_i, residues a_i mod q_i s.t. â_i (a_i + q_i â¤) â {1,2,â¦,L} for L > \frac{\log\log Q \log\log\log\log Q}{(\log\log \log Q)^2} \log Q.
move.rationale: Provides the key covering system with coprime moduli for CRT solvability to force prime-free [N+1,N+L] for N â¡ -a_i mod q_i âi (solved mod Q).
move.test_plan: Construct explicit small covering (e.g., mod 2,3,4,5,6,8,12,24 covering length ~10); scale to asymptotic via greedy or known constructions; verify union covers interval.
dependencies: Chinese Remainder Theorem for coprime moduli
move.expected_effect: Advances Milestone 2, enabling N = M Q - r solving the system for prime sieve.
move.why_this_applies_here: Euclid case constructs N avoiding primes mod prod (covers all residues but one); transfers to interval covering with density control for iterated logs.
move.how_to_falsify_fast: Attempt explicit q_i up to prod Q=10^6; if max L/Q << log Q / polyloglog Q, construction inadequate.

payload.query: Milestone 1: reduction from prime-free interval to p_{n+1}-p_n bound Milestone 2: valid CRT covering lemma with pairwise-coprime structure Milestone 3: parameter-growth lemma linking N, Q, and n Is it true that, for any $C>0$, there are infinitely many $n$ such that\[p_{n+1}-p_n> C\frac{\log\log n\log\log\log\log n}{(\log\log \log n)^2}\log n?\] formalize: â pairwise coprime q_1,â¦,q_k â¥ 2 with Q = â q_i, residues a_i mod q_i s.t. â_i (a_i + q_i â¤) â {1,2,â¦,L} for L > \frac{\log\log Q \log\log\log\log Q}{(\log\log \log Q)^2} \log Q.

move.claim: As N â â, log Ï(N) â¼ log N and log log Ï(N) â¼ log log N (with all higher iterated logs asymptotically equivalent).
move.rationale: Prime Number Theorem implies Ï(N) â¼ N / log N, so log Ï(N) = log N - log log N + O(1) â¼ log N; higher logs converge similarly due to slow growth.
move.test_plan: Symbolically expand using PNT error terms O(N / log^2 N); verify numerically for N up to 10^12 using known Ï(N) tables.
move.expected_effect: Provides asymptotic bridge for transferring interval lengths L(N) to gap bounds at n = Ï(N).
move.why_this_applies_here: No direct match; general PNT tool analogous to density arguments in sample_solved_001.
required_preconditions: PNT: Ï(N) â¼ N / log N
move.how_to_falsify_fast: Compute log Ï(10^6)/log(10^6) â 0.85 > 0.8; if deviates from 1 for larger N, falsify.
